message:
feat(ui): normalize source filters through all TUI display, persistence, and saved view paths

Source filter normalization in display:
- In the source filter menu overlay, compare each menu item's filter against
  normalize_source_filter(current filter) instead of the raw filter, so that
  a SourceFilter::SourceId("  LOCAL  ") correctly shows the asterisk on the
  matching "local" row.
- In the search footer HUD, normalize the source filter before rendering the
  "src:..." scope label, preventing untrimmed whitespace from appearing in
  the status bar.

Source filter normalization in persistence:
- In persisted_state_file_from_state, normalize each saved view's source_filter
  and the analytics_source_filter before serializing to JSON, so persisted state
  files always contain canonical filter values (trimmed, LOCAL->local).
- This prevents drift where a view saved with "  LOCAL  " would deserialize
  differently than one saved with "local".

Source filter normalization in saved view save/load:
- ViewSaved message handler: normalize the source filter before storing it in
  the saved views list, so saving a view with whitespace-padded source filter
  stores the canonical form.
- ViewLoaded message handler: normalize the source filter when restoring from
  a saved view, so loading a legacy view with non-canonical source filter
  applies the correct canonical filter.

Test coverage:
- Add persisted_state_file_from_state_normalizes_source_filter_strings test
  verifying that whitespace-padded and case-variant source filters are
  normalized in both saved views and analytics filter during serialization.
- Add view_saved_normalizes_source_filter_before_storing_saved_view test.
- Add view_loaded_normalizes_source_filter_from_saved_view test.
- Add search_footer_hud_normalizes_trimmed_source_filter_scope test verifying
  the footer renders "src:work-laptop" not "src:  work-laptop  ".
- Add source_filter_menu_overlay_marks_normalized_source_filter_active test.
- Hoist buffer_to_text import to module-level use statement, removing redundant
  per-test imports in compact_density, density_effective, results_surface, and
  results_metadata tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
